data_source: Historical space weather data (Celestrak SW-All.csv) matched to TEP-EFA flyby fits
derivation: Space weather correlation analysis with n=3 fitted flybys; ±50% uncertainty accounts for statistical limitations and systematic errors in the space weather proxy
recommended_action: Validate with larger sample size (n>10) from future flyby measurements

analysis.data_source: Historical space weather data (Celestrak SW-All.csv) matched to TEP-EFA flyby fits
analysis.derivation: Space weather correlation analysis with n=3 fitted flybys; ±50% uncertainty accounts for statistical limitations and systematic errors in the space weather proxy
analysis.recommended_action: Validate with larger sample size (n>10) from future flyby measurements

correlation.data_source: Historical space weather data (Celestrak SW-All.csv) matched to TEP-EFA flyby fits
correlation.derivation: Pearson correlation r=0.9803 between space weather plasma index (P_env = F107 * (1 + Kp/100)) and fitted beta values; n=3 samples; ±50% uncertainty accounts for small sample statistical limitations and systematic errors in space weather proxy
correlation.recommended_action: Validate with larger sample size (n>10) from future flyby measurements; consider multi-mission coordinated campaigns to increase statistical power
correlation.interpretation: Insufficient data (n=3) for reliable correlation assessment
correlation.statistical_power_note: With n=3, minimum detectable correlation at 80% power is r≈0.8. Current sample is underpowered for definitive correlation testing.

catalog_context.error: No usable KP_SUM value in Celestrak SW-All for 2029-08-01
No usable KP_SUM value in Celestrak SW-All for 2030-02-01

concordance.data_source: Wide binary R_s transition analysis (Paper 6, UCD)
concordance.derivation: Wide binary concordance analysis; Temporal Shear Suppression index n = 3.0 represents power-law dependence of scalar force suppression on ambient density; ±50% uncertainty accounts for galactic environment variations
concordance.recommended_action: Validate against independent galactic rotation curve analysis and GNSS calibration consistency check

wide_binary_concordance.data_source: Wide binary R_s transition analysis (Paper 6, UCD)
wide_binary_concordance.derivation: Temporal Shear Suppression index n = 3.0 represents the power-law dependence of scalar force suppression on ambient density; derived from wide binary R_s transitions where orbital period ratios deviate from Newtonian predictions
wide_binary_concordance.recommended_action: Validate against independent galactic rotation curve analysis and GNSS calibration consistency check
wide_binary_concordance.consistency: Wide-binary screening scale is documented separately; flyby beta vs P_env correlation is not significant at the current fitted-sample size.

statistical_notes: With n=4 primary detections, correlation analysis is exploratory only
I² > 99.9% heterogeneity may reflect genuine modulation OR model limitations
Space weather correlation requires validation with n>10 flybys
Do not cite correlations from this step as confirmed results
reviewer_warnings: r=0.64, p=0.36 on n=4 is not statistically significant
Remove ionospheric correlation claims from main manuscript
